reviews.review_description:
I want to give this game 10 stars 
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
— 5 stars are simply not enough!

This is easily the best snooker experience I’ve ever had in VR, and honestly the closest thing to playing real snooker in real life. The physics are incredibly natural and realistic, and every shot feels smooth, accurate, and satisfying.

The cueing system is absolutely genius. It’s designed in such a smart and immersive way that it genuinely feels like holding and controlling a real cue. You can clearly see how much care and attention the developers put into every detail.

Without a doubt, this is one of the top-rated sports games on Meta Quest. Whether you’re a casual player or a serious cue sports fan, this game delivers an amazing experience.

I highly recommend this game to all fans of cue sports. It is 100% worth buying!

Snooker games are very rare in vr, But this game proves it can be done and this game is absolutely outstanding. From the size of the table to the physics and sounds, This is as good as playing the real thing. 2 things are needed, When the ball is near a cushion it's difficult to get your hand to go where you want it to, And there is no AI game mode available.  But everything else is perfect, A must buy game.

reviews.review_title: Love the game, hate the trigger issue
reviews.review_description:
Great game, but shot initiation needs urgent fixing!
I really enjoy this game - the physics and overall experience are excellent. However, there's one critical issue that's extremely frustrating:
Shot initiation problem: When you pull the trigger to start a shot, the cue often immediately moves forward and hits the cue ball unintentionally, resulting in a miss and foul. This happens far too often and ruins the experience.
Solution needed: When the trigger is pressed, the cue should NOT be able to move forward immediately. It must first be pulled back a certain distance before it can move forward to strike. This would completely solve the accidental shot problem.

AI opponents would be fantastic for solo practice
Additional game modes (8-ball, 9-ball) would add great variety

Despite the shot control issue, the core gameplay is very good. If the developers fix the trigger/shot initiation mechanic, this would easily be a 5-star game. For now, it's a solid 3-4 stars with huge potential.
Recommendation: Buy it if you love snooker, but be aware of the shot control frustration until it's patched.

reviews.review_title: As close to snooker as it gets
reviews.review_description:
The addition of the real cue alignment makes this the most immersive experience I've had so far. Your angles, cueing action and power need to be practically the same as you would need on a real table, especially if you take aim assist off. Very enjoyable to play. 
All I would say it is lacking is an option to have a solo play mode which tracks your score. 
If this company could develop a pool game with the same physics and cue alignment it would be by far the best on the market.

reviews.review_title: Cue tracking is a game changer
reviews.review_description: While I always thought the physics were good in this game, it was hard for me to play with controllers. Now that I can attach a controller to my cue, the game is superb. It makes such a difference. If you play snooker in real life, it's definitely worth getting this now. The only issue is that there isn't an adapter designed specifically for the game yet, but there are options discussed in the games discord.

reviews.review_title: The most realistic snooker game ever
reviews.review_description:
Having a 12 foot snooker table in your living room is ridiculously good, i watch football etc wjile playing in pass through, irs the snooker game for proper snooker game enthusiasts
Be nice if it counted your breaks in freeplay mode, and if you had 1 toilet break per game if required but so so good! In my eyes the best game on the quest 👌
Great game just takes a bit of getting used to

reviews.review_title: Great, but I need some help.
reviews.review_description: I've seen the videos on how to use a real cue with this game, but the alignment with the virtual cue seems pretty hit and miss.  Could someone give me a proper tutorial on how to do it?  Besides that the game is great.

developer_response.body:
Hi Dan,
Make sure you join our Discord server you'll find lots of useful information on real cue attachment tips and ideas from many other players. Link below:
https://discord.gg/t3yTJv3xw5
Cheers!

reviews.review_description:
Great table physics and graphics.   Now table rotation is added it’s much better to align shots. 

Just need a voice option and some sort of league status and it will be 10/10 difficult without the aim assist but that makes it tough just like the real game 👍

reviews.review_title: As good as it can get
reviews.review_description: I bought this for Quest 3 thinking for some reason that headset could track a controller behind itself. Unfortunately it cannot so you are always stuck playing “trombone” style which is such a shame as everything else looks and feels perfect. Not the developers fault at all it’s a hardware limitation but feel if you’re a keen snooker player you should be aware that you won’t be able to strike the ball with your arms in the same position as on a real table. Maybe think of it like each shot is a “rest” shot!
Best snooker game on VR at the moment still improvement to be maid but overall great!

reviews.review_title: Afyer updates, sill not for me
reviews.review_description:
My friend told me this had recieved some patches so i installed to try again, Honestly it has improved somewhat and "when" it works it actually feels great and is enjoyable, 

However the areas i took most issue with before have not been addressed/changed, lining up a shot is still a difficult & such a hassle (even leaning on a real world table) the ghost aiming hand is awful and makes aiming an annoyance & it quickly tests your good mood, also the sidespin on the ball i personally feel is Extremely exaggerated & you combine that with the difficulty/un-intuitiveness of lining up a shot and its not enjoyable even to take a shot regardless of miss or potting a ball, 

Not a fan of the dev or whomever responds to the reviews of this game if not him himself, (does not take negetive criticism well at all) but credit where its due and this game has been getting updates which is good to see regardless, its not for me so be aware of the issues i outlined, 

I believe there's another pool/snooker game coming out this month so im hoping thats more to my liking but also if that has "some" better mechanics then Unlimited Snooker then i hope this dev would be open minded about possible positive changes to help improve this game for those that want to enjoy it but cannot due to control issues or etc

Having had a meta quest 3 for a good few months now, I’ve been trying as many sports games as I can. 

Eleven table tennis I thought would take some beating, but this game right here, it beats it in my opinion. 

The graphics are lovely! The sound is spot on and the weight of the balls, the heaviness of the contact, its all brilliant. 

Playing with a friend works absolutely flawlessly also which is great. 


There was a second long part of this review, where I described a few issues, but I’ve had to edit that out because….

Just as good as the game is, the developers are just as good.

I messaged them via email with my issues, and they responded within half an hour! Were really friendly, and helped with my issues, which I must mention, were my fault. 

The game is a joy, and I cannot possibly see what game can be released that beats this, for the immersion and the true to life feeling it gives. 

I think about going on to play my brother after work, and genuinely feel like I’m looking forward to a few frames of real snooker. 

The great safety battles etc. 

Just to mention it has 4 levels of assists(aiming lines, ghost ball)

So you can start high and work your way down as you get better. 

The biggest compliment I can give this game, and eleven table tennis is similar…. 

If you played this at a mates…. You’d absolutely look to buy the meta quest just for this game. 

SUPERB!!

reviews.review_title: Finally! The game I've been waiting for.
reviews.review_description:
This is a straight forward, no-nonsense game with no superfluous arcadey gimmicks. Just simulation snooker, plain and simple. I’m a few weeks into playing this & I’m hooked. The look, sounds, and feel of this game seem very authentic.

There's options to suit players of all abilities. Aiming assists are there should you need them. I’ve never played on a full size snooker table, the bulk of my experience is just VR pool games but I’m personally relishing the challenge learning from scratch with no assists. Snooker’s not an easy game so the learning curve is steeper and it’s so satisfying when you start to see improvements. I’ve started playing every day now & I can’t recommend this game enough. I feel it’s a must-have for any cue sports enthusiast. 

There’s Free Play mode which includes practice routines. At the time of writing these comprise of the standard Line Up, the T drill, the X drill, and Stephen Hendry’s Tough Table. Also you can save multiple custom set-ups of your own.

There’s a Pass and Play option, though I just use that to practice without the temptation of an undo button & to keep track of any possible high breaks. Then there’s Online Public and Private match options. 

The developer's had the good sense to make Public matches have a timer and the other person’s “avatar” is hidden during your shot. So no having to deal with the usual trolls that can plague games by trying to distract you. Then in Private games, no timer & both players’ avatars can been seen at all times, trusting that as you’re choosing opponents here you’re going to play with respect. People can also spectate private matches. I’ve not tried this yet, but I would assume this could be useful if the spectator can record gameplay, as again I would assume that they are hidden from the person in play at the very least so they can get in optimum positions to record the gameplay. 

Looking forward to where the developer takes this game in the future. 
So much potential here, I love it!

Good game everything is good but please add how to play without the e controllers while holding any stick as an example snooker cue then play how you will play in real life no game have ever done it if you do it it will be the best

While the concept is really good you have some flaws in the game. one really annoying flaw is you can't really play the ball when it is against the cushion the cue wont lock on to it and it is really awkward to lift the cue since the cue holding position is also awkward. another issue is the ghost ball tangent line is way off it doesn't even aim where the ball is supposed to go keep working on it mainly focusing on the cue action and ball positioning with the ghost ball and tangent line. once those are fixed I will rate better

Honestly. This is right up there with Eleven TT as one of the greatest sports sims on VR.  I would say even pips Miracle Pool (unless snooker not your thing). The mechanics of this game. The ball physics. The shot selection. All 100% perfect.
For those that are struggling with the aim then you are simply applying too much English which will throw the ball off. Choose orbital cueing (better than Natural) and dont apply any spin until you understand the shot mechanics.
There isnt a one player v cpu but it doesnt matter much. If the developer could add some scenarios or other modes that would be even better, but as it is... It is a MASTERPIECE.

Cueing and lining up a shot are almost impossible with the ghost hand 
I really did want to love the game, but ￼ every time I try to shoot a ball my stick flips up and down sideways just moves all around. I cannot line up a shot for the life of me. I have tried different lighting, different table heights different settings. I can’t seem to figure out what I’m doing wrong i’ve uninstalled it tried to reinstall ￼￼￼
￼￼ besides this I love trying to play the game so if anybody can help with telling me what I might be doing wrong, I’d appreciate it ￼

developer_response.body:
Hi Matt, thank you very much for trying out this game. If you're usure about the controls you can always revisit the tutorials from the main menu. Also, here's a link to a Discord server some players have set up so you might find some help and online players in there:
https://discord.gg/KAfYPVck
Or you can always drop me an email at:
contact@spsoftwareproductions.com

Cheers.

reviews.review_description:
This is an amazing game 
The graphics are amazing too 
Me and my nephew play regularly on the online match play .
The only thing that's missing is when opponents foul when snookered the option for force retake is not available. And maybe ranking tournament would be fantastic for online and AI opponents for single player mode .
Hope the devs keep up fantastic work and maybe try implement these features 👍

This is the best looking table i have ever seen in a VR glasses and i had them all since Oculus rift and my only games are Pool and snooker games  and i also go to local Snooker and pool in my neighbourhood.I started playing Virtual pool 1   in .95 i think and then  nr.2,3,4,    and now mostly Shooterspool on the PC and i often played Sportsbar VR  .  Now what this game needs very badly in my opinion is an easier Control on the table alignment.Using a remote control to walk back and forth around the table is nauseating and uncomfortable .And causes you to stop playing very quickly and go somewhere else.However, if you could move the table with your hands just by holding down buttons or even 2 buttons on each remote control and turn the table however you want, both forward and backward, up and down, then this would be perfect and the game would be very popular and sought after.I hope this is constructive criticism and to the producers, this is a high quality game

This is a fantastic game and so realistic. It takes a little bit of time to get the hang of the controls bit it's a realistic sim so that's understandable and it's very rewarding once you learn how to play.

I love Miracle Pool and really enjoyed the Snooker game on that but it doesn't implement the proper rules but this game does.

I haven't played online yet as I'm not good enough and feel if I met someone my own standard it would take hours to complet a frame!

It would be amazing if you guys could add a bot to play against in practice, I'd happily pay for that update. Also it would be good if you could choose to adjust the brightness of the green table as I find the contrast between the green and red quite harsh on the eyes after concentrating hard on long shots etc for a long time.

One last thing, you have to change the control scheme or at least give people the choice before starting the tutorial. Pressing left to move the table right and up to move the table and then trying to do diagonals that way down made it by far the most frustrating tutorial I've ever done. It's a good job you can change it once you get out of the tutorial and you must have known people wouldn't like it as you tell people at the start of the tutorial they can change it later. Who thought that was a good idea?

Despite that I'm still giving it 5 stars as once I get good on it I have a feeling it will be my favourite game, it's just so realistic and the physics are incredible.
It gets tricky with the controls but when you do it’s a blast. I think it’s worth the money and hoping for future updates to make it better

30 minutes to get thru tutorial was nearly enough to ask for a refund, it needs a bypass option as it wouldn’t let me hit a ball in one screen & I had to keep starting over till it fixed itself
Actual game is great
Maybe add a YouTube screen or something for the ones who want to watch music videos etc, the locomotion could be easier as I had a hard time getting the table in position for some shots.
All in all very good 
Edit* I managed to put my hand anywhere on the table, don’t ask me how , is it a cheat mode? Idk it’s not a setting I could find in the options
Edit* it would be great to have a arcade option where u can put your hand anywhere on the table unlike sim mode 
edit* be good to have a discord for a wish list/improvements but as we haven’t I’ll say it here, we need a practice setting for different ball lineups so we don’t need to move every ball every time in a line, a top down screen to plan your next shot & maybe some nice environments. Anything else I can think of I’ll leave it here hopefully to be read by the makers
Edit there is a discord & the dev is very involved in updates and feature requests, once you play for a bit it’s easy to move and play just like irl, it’s the best game on Quest without a doubt
Buy buy buy
Physics great feels very realistic, but needs ai bots to play, a youtube screen and struggle to get a online game

reviews.review_title: Just updated my review from 2 stars to 4
reviews.review_description:
Just tried this, and all I can say is it looks great, the scale is spot on but the mechanics are terrible, moving around the table is so labored and so time consuming it takes away from what could be a great game, the aiming with your bridge hand is really bad, irl life the bridge hand is used primarily to steady the cue all the aiming is done with the back hand so when playing this game it's really hard to aim with the bridge hand I found I was all over the place with it, it really is such a pity as this could have been amazing, but I'll keep the game in the hope that they (the devs) sort it out, so I'm afraid it's a two stars but it they sort out the aiming it probably be a five 👍

I've just had to update my review from 2 stars to 4 stars, after the Devs latest patch the game is so so much improved, fully deserves 4 stars maybe even more, there are a couple of things I'd like to see altered like the side/english power but all in all a brilliant snooker sim now, very well done Devs 💯👍
Really good physics, and a decent amount of options. But this game really needs Ai/bot opponents to play against... please devs make it happen.
Great game if you get the control right, if the control was the same as miracle pool this would be incredible

Recent update has taken this up a notch. Whilst the controls may scare away some casuals, there is no doubt this game is art and a pure snooker simulation. 

Massive props to dev for his commitment and skill for working and this and focusing on a real snooker sim experience. The little touches and attention to detail is incredible. Physics, sounds, visuals, immersion 10/10. Keep up the great work. 

Thanks

I am a regular snooker player, I have had the Oculus 2 for some time always hopping a good snooker game would come along at some point. So I was very pleased to see this game launched, It looks amazing but it seems impossible to actually play, having tried for a couple hours to get a grip on the cue I could not get the hang of it. So I am not sure what the problem is Maybe it's not ideal for the Oculus 2.  I can only give it a 1 star for now I will persevere a while longer and 
review again.

developer_response.body:
Hi Peter, with the Quest 2 and the Natural cue control scheme, if you try to place your cueing hand (back hand) too far behind your body, it would lose tracking quite easily. This is unfortunately a limitation with the quest 2 tracking capability. To overcome this, you can use the Orbit cue control scheme instead, which only utilize the front hand (bridge hand) for cue control and aiming. Or if you want to stick with the Natural cue control scheme, you may try to use what experienced VR cue sports game players called the "trombone" method, which is basically control the cue with both hands in front of your body.
I would strongly recommend you to go through the tutorials again with different options to find the controls that work for you. Hope it helps. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: I have waited so long for a full table snooker game,just purchased this it really feels great seeing a snooker table in my home lol can you lock the aim before playing the shot and any advice about getting a fixed table I don’t have a square one as we have a glass round dining table any tips would be appreciated but a lock to shot would be great. Well done devs and Ty —at last been playing a few on line matches and it’s brill ,please add more assets into the game oh and build me a bar in my room hic hic cheers

I’m genuinely impressed. The gameplay is excellent — smooth, immersive, and surprisingly realistic, right down to the angle you hold the cue.It takes a little time to get fully used to the controls, but once you do, it feels incredibly natural.

The graphics are almost top-notch for a quest game, and the snap turning works well, though there’s still a bit of room for refinement in future updates. What really stood out to me was discovering features I initially thought were missing, only to find out they were actually there all along. That kind of depth and attention to detail really impressed me.

For a first release, this game is remarkably polished. It already delivers a strong experience and clearly has huge potential as it continues to evolve. Highly recommended for any snooker fan or VR sports enthusiast.

developer_response.body:
Hi spannerflat,
Thank you so much for your support. More updates/improvements will come so stayed tuned. Cheers!

reviews.review_title: best digital snooker game by far
reviews.review_description:
I'm going to give this game 5 stars because it feels so real and the hand on the table feel is a game changer. All that said I still what to give some feedback to the developers which hopefully can improve the game further.
The aiming system on the free setting(the most natural) is a little cumbersome, let me try and explain. When you try and position the tip to where you want to add side and then you aim to pot when you then look back at the cue ball its almost moved parallel with the table and has become way off. IRL once the side is positioned gentle tweaks with cue arm would adjust the aim. to replicate this I would like to see once you have positioned your side on the cueball be able to lock it. then adjust your aim with your dominant hand and lock it then concentrate on the strength when taking the shot. all that said I love it

If you've ever played snooker a few times, you'll know how difficult it is to string two pots together. And that's without considering position on your next shot!


Unlimited Snooker replicates the sport impeccably. The physics, the weight of the balls, the visuals and even the sounds are perfect. Controls have been improved upon since launch 4 weeks ago. They felt good before but updates in the past week or two, with the addition of the natural cueing method and free movement around the table, mean they now feel perfect, to me anyway.


The rules are all there. Foul and a miss and free ball rule are there as you'd expect, which add to the genuine nature of the game.


The sounds are spot on. The ball and cushion thuds, and the pocket sounds, from a light trickle into a pocket to the slamming of the leather when a ball hits the back of the pocket. This is something which manhy dedicated snooker games in years gone by haven't quite got right. But Ultimate Snooker has nailed it. It might be a small thing, but it's very pleasing when it's done correctly, as it has been here.


A complaint of the game has been that it's so difficult. But it should be! Because snooker is very difficult at the best of times. The pockets are very tight, but to me that's correct.


What was there when the game first launched was great, but needed some touching up in terms of controls and table movement. This has recently been addressed.


Really hoping to see stats pages and the addition of spectators to online games, as well as mic chat in online play and some form of ELO/ranking system. Maybe players can have a profile page in future which lists stats such as highest break, average break, etc.


However, the essentials of snooker are all there right now as it is. How it plays, how it feels, how it sounds. Miracle Pool "tried" to do snooker and fell a long way short.


Ultimate Snooker has it all down to a tee. Sensational effort, from what I believe is the work of one person!

I'm enjoying the game, it's pretty realistic and generally is a good representation of Snooker. I think the game has improved since the recent update, but (and this could be me still getting used to the game), I still feel the cue mechanics could be improved, i.e. more flexibility/options with how the cue is held in both hands, and also the speed in which the game transitions from one player to the other when playing in two player mode, this is sluggish. But, other than improving the cue mechanics as mentioned, my main ask from the Dev's is to make a deeper tutorial to get the best from the game, including things like - practice modes, challenges, ai opponents, best table dimensions (min. requirements etc.), stats (highest break, long pot success etc.) and table positioning in certain areas and more general on screen examples of typical/various shots explaining how you may need move around the table to get the best position - more user friendly etc., saying all that, it's really good already so excited to see what the future updates are.

U cant move white Ball with Hand in t
he D-Zone !?  No  " Ball in Hand "  ?

developer_response.body: Hi dogglounge, you can move the cue ball by choosing the "Move Cue Ball" option from the menu. Also with the new update you can move the ball with the hand instead of the ray. Also the move cue ball mode will be automatically enabled if it is "ball in hand" in match play. Enjoy! :)

reviews.review_title: Very well thought through snooker sim
reviews.review_description: With the latest update the controls are amazing - natural cueing with two hands and you are able to position both hands independently as you cue.  The game has lots of good features for snooker like break counts, scoreboards, auto respot in practice, proper and beautiful snooker pockets.  Physics seems excellent (swerve and deflection is well modelled - cushions react very naturally).It pretty much forces you to play from outside the table using a rest when needed - very clever implementation. Cues look beautiful (limited options, but very realistic) and very realistic.  Multiplayer and local pass n play game modes work well with no lag in multiplayer.  No voice chat but you can host a separate meta call with friends if needed.  Congrats on an amazing snooker simulation!  Looking forward to seeing how it continues to grow.

They got so much right with this snooker sim. table, balls and pockets are all perfect size. I even love the sounds. The physics seem to be spot on.The only  downside is the controls dont feel natural or intuitive. Moving your bridge hand to aim is weird. also moving the table around is a bit of  pain. Still giving it five stars because its the best implementation of snooker we got in vr so far.

developer_response.body:
Hi Deucejuice, thank you so much for showing support for this game.
We are aware of that our aiming system might not suit everybody at the moment. In our minds we want to enable our players to truly experience the tactical side of Snooker, even for beginners.
We will keep exploring new systems that might feel more natural for more experienced players.
This game is just getting started, so stay tuned :)

reviews.review_description:
Reassessed my original review after spending some more time with it. Controls take getting used to but they offer some advantages for fine tuning the cue hitting the cueball.

There are still issues with the how you move the table around but there are workarounds.

This is most fun in multiplayer mode but the game is very good and promises a lot with future updates.

developer_response.body: Hi Eqvinox, this new table alignment system means to match your real table in your room with the virtual snooker table, edge to edge, corner to corner, so that you could rest your bridge hand on the table while you stroke just like in real life. This might take some time to get used to but it's for the benefit of making your experience more realistic. You might want to leave more space behind you and around both corners of your table for the best experience. Or if you wish, you can "push" your table away by realigning the table from the options menu. It is just not encouraged as players are meant to play around the table not "inside" or "on" the table. For both bridge hand stroke and rest stroke, you can still cue with the same hand. You absolutely don't have to swap controllers. If you are not so sure you can always go through the tutorials again. On a side note, leaving your opponent with no space to place their bridge hand on the table by positioning the cue ball near a cushion is a real tactic in Snooker! Cheers.

reviews.review_title: The best VR Snooker Sim so far
reviews.review_description:
As a sim fan, this game is great, and with a better more customisable control system you will get five stars from me!
You need a discord so that your game community can help you improve the game experience.
excellent mechanics, very realistic simulation. recommended
Graphics physics all good only down side to me is movement, smooth movement still needs a little tweaking
